name: kubectl
description: Kubernetes command-line tool for managing clusters

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Display one or many resources

args.name: <resource>
args.description: Resource type (pods, services, deployments, nodes, etc.)

options.name: --show-labels
options.description: Show all labels as the last column

options.name: --ignore-not-found
options.description: Treat 'resource not found' as successful retrieval

options.name: --raw
options.description: Raw URI to request from the server

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.description: Apply a configuration to a resource by file or stdin

options.name: --filename
options.description: Filename, directory, or URL to apply

options.name: --prune
options.description: Automatically delete resources not in the applied set

options.name: --force
options.description: Delete and re-create resource if needed

options.name: --field-manager
options.description: Name of the manager for server-side apply

options.name: --force-conflicts
options.description: Force apply even with field conflicts (server-side)

options.name: --record
options.description: Record current kubectl command in resource annotation

examples:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: my-config
data:
  key: value
EOF

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete resources by filenames, stdin, resources and names, or by resources and label selector

options.name: --filename
options.description: Filename, directory, or URL for resource manifests

options.name: --all
options.description: Delete all resources of the specified type

options.name: --grace-period
options.description: Seconds to wait before force deletion (-1 for default)

options.name: --force
options.description: Immediately remove resources from API and bypass graceful deletion

options.name: --wait
options.description: Wait for resources to be fully deleted (default true)

options.name: --ignore-not-found
options.description: Treat 'resource not found' errors as successful

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Time to wait before giving up on deletion

subcommands.name: describe
subcommands.description: Show details of a specific resource or group of resources

options.name: --show-events
options.description: Show events associated with the resource (default true)

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Print the logs for a container in a pod

options.name: --previous
options.description: Print logs from the previous instance of the container

options.name: --since
options.description: Show logs newer than a relative duration (e.g. 5m, 1h)

options.name: --since-time
options.description: Show logs after a specific RFC3339 timestamp

options.name: --tail
options.description: Number of lines to show from the end

options.name: --all-containers
options.description: Get logs from all containers in the pod

options.name: --max-log-requests
options.description: Max number of concurrent log requests (when using -l)

options.name: --limit-bytes
options.description: Maximum bytes of logs to return

options.name: --prefix
options.description: Prefix each line with pod name and container name

options.name: --ignore-errors
options.description: If watching/following, allow errors to be ignored

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in a container

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Only print output from the remote session

examples: kubectl exec my-pod -- printenv | grep DATABASE

subcommands.name: port-forward
subcommands.description: Forward one or more local ports to a pod

options.name: --pod-running-timeout
options.description: Timeout for waiting for at least one pod to be running

subcommands.name: create deployment
subcommands.description: Create a deployment with the specified name

options.name: --save-config
options.description: Save the configuration of the resource in annotation

subcommands.name: create service clusterip
subcommands.description: Create a ClusterIP service

options.name: --clusterip
options.description: Assign a static ClusterIP or set to 'None'

examples: kubectl create service clusterip my-svc --tcp=80:8080 --dry-run=client -o yaml

subcommands.name: create service nodeport
subcommands.description: Create a NodePort service

subcommands.name: create service loadbalancer
subcommands.description: Create a LoadBalancer service
examples: kubectl create service loadbalancer my-svc --tcp=443:8443 --dry-run=client -o yaml

subcommands.name: create namespace
subcommands.description: Create a namespace with the specified name

subcommands.name: create configmap
subcommands.description: Create a configmap from literal values, files, or directories

options.name: --from-file
options.description: File or directory to populate configmap from

options.name: --from-env-file
options.description: Env file to populate configmap from

options.name: --append-hash
options.description: Append a hash to the configmap name

subcommands.name: create secret generic
subcommands.description: Create a secret from literal values, files, or directories

options.name: --from-env-file
options.description: Env file to populate secret from

options.name: --append-hash
options.description: Append a hash to the secret name

examples: kubectl create secret generic my-secret --from-literal=password=s3cr3t -n production
kubectl create secret generic db-creds --from-literal=DB_PASSWORD=pass --dry-run=client -o yaml

subcommands.name: create secret tls
subcommands.description: Create a TLS secret from a certificate and key pair

options.name: --key
options.description: Path to TLS private key file

examples: kubectl create secret tls my-tls --cert=cert.pem --key=key.pem -n production

subcommands.name: edit
subcommands.description: Edit a resource on the server using the default editor

options.name: --output
options.description: Output format for the saved resource

options.name: --field-manager
options.description: Name of the field manager used for server-side apply

subcommands.name: scale
subcommands.description: Set a new size for a deployment, replica set, or stateful set

options.name: --current-replicas
options.description: Precondition for expected current replica count

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Timeout for waiting for the scaling to complete

options.name: --all
options.description: Select all resources of the specified type

subcommands.name: rollout status
subcommands.description: Show the status of a rollout

options.name: --watch
options.description: Watch the rollout status until done (default true)

options.name: --revision
options.description: Pin to a specific revision for showing status

subcommands.name: rollout history
subcommands.description: View rollout history of a resource

options.name: --revision
options.description: See details of a specific revision

subcommands.name: rollout undo
subcommands.description: Undo a previous rollout

options.name: --to-revision
options.description: Revision number to rollback to (0 = previous)

subcommands.name: rollout restart
subcommands.description: Restart a resource (triggers a rolling restart)

subcommands.name: expose
subcommands.description: Expose a resource as a new Kubernetes service

options.name: --port
options.description: Port the service should listen on

options.name: --target-port
options.description: Port on the container to route to

options.name: --external-ip
options.description: External IP address to set for the service

options.name: --cluster-ip
options.description: ClusterIP to assign ('' for auto, 'None' for headless)

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Create and run a particular image in a pod

options.name: --command
options.description: Use command mode (pass command and args after --)

options.name: --rm
options.description: Delete pod after it exits (with --attach or --stdin)

options.name: --attach
options.description: Wait for the pod to start and attach to it

options.name: --overrides
options.description: JSON override for the generated object

examples: kubectl run load-gen --image=busybox --restart=Never -- /bin/sh -c 'while true; do wget -qO- http://my-svc; done'

subcommands.name: attach
subcommands.description: Attach to a process that is already running inside an existing container

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Only print output from the remote session

subcommands.name: cp
subcommands.description: Copy files and directories to and from containers

options.name: --no-preserve
options.description: Do not preserve ownership and permissions

options.name: --retries
options.description: Number of retries on network errors

subcommands.name: top nodes
subcommands.description: Display resource (CPU/memory) usage of nodes

options.name: --show-capacity
options.description: Show node resource capacity in addition to usage

options.name: --use-protocol-buffers
options.description: Use protocol buffers to access Metrics API

subcommands.name: top pods
subcommands.description: Display resource (CPU/memory) usage of pods

options.name: --use-protocol-buffers
options.description: Use protocol buffers to access Metrics API

subcommands.name: config view
subcommands.description: Display merged kubeconfig settings or a specified kubeconfig file

options.name: --minify
options.description: Remove info not used by current-context

options.name: --raw
options.description: Display raw byte data and sensitive fields

options.name: --merge
options.description: Merge the full kubeconfig hierarchy (default true)

subcommands.name: config use-context
subcommands.description: Set the current context in kubeconfig

subcommands.name: config get-contexts
subcommands.description: Display one or many contexts from the kubeconfig file

subcommands.name: config set-context
subcommands.description: Set a context entry in kubeconfig

options.name: --namespace
options.description: Default namespace for the context entry

subcommands.name: config current-context
subcommands.description: Display the current context from kubeconfig

subcommands.name: label
subcommands.description: Update the labels on a resource

args.name: <labels>
args.description: Label key=value pairs (use key- to remove)

options.name: --all
options.description: Select all resources of the specified type

options.name: --list
options.description: List current labels for the resource

examples: kubectl label pod my-pod tier=frontend env=prod

subcommands.name: annotate
subcommands.description: Update the annotations on a resource

args.name: <annotations>
args.description: Annotation key=value pairs (use key- to remove)

options.name: --all
options.description: Select all resources of the specified type

examples: kubectl annotate pod my-pod description='My web server'
kubectl annotate deployment my-app kubernetes.io/change-cause='image update to v2'

subcommands.name: patch
subcommands.description: Update fields of a resource using strategic merge, JSON merge, or JSON patch

options.name: --patch-file
options.description: Path to a file containing the patch

subcommands.name: taint
subcommands.description: Update the taints on one or more nodes

options.name: --overwrite
options.description: Overwrite existing taints with matching key

subcommands.name: cordon
subcommands.description: Mark a node as unschedulable

subcommands.name: uncordon
subcommands.description: Mark a node as schedulable

subcommands.name: drain
subcommands.description: Drain a node in preparation for maintenance

options.name: --force
options.description: Continue even if there are pods not managed by a controller

options.name: --grace-period
options.description: Seconds to wait for pod termination

options.name: --delete-emptydir-data
options.description: Continue even if there are pods using emptyDir

options.name: --timeout
options.description: Time to wait before giving up

options.name: --pod-selector
options.description: Label selector to filter pods on the node

options.name: --disable-eviction
options.description: Force drain using delete even if eviction is supported

options.name: --skip-wait-for-delete-timeout
options.description: Skip waiting for pods with DeletionTimestamp > N seconds

options.name: --chunk-size
options.description: Number of pods processed at once during eviction

subcommands.name: api-resources
subcommands.description: Print the supported API resources on the server

options.name: --api-group
options.description: Limit to resources in the specified API group

options.name: --namespaced
options.description: If false, non-namespaced resources are listed; if true, namespaced resources (default true)

options.name: --verbs
options.description: Limit output to resources that support specified verbs

options.name: --cached
options.description: Use the cached list of resources

subcommands.name: api-versions
subcommands.description: Print the supported API versions on the server

subcommands.name: explain
subcommands.description: Get documentation of a resource or field

options.name: --api-version
options.description: Specify API version to explain (e.g. apps/v1)

subcommands.name: auth can-i
subcommands.description: Check whether an action is allowed in the cluster

args.name: <verb>
args.description: API verb (get, list, create, delete, update, patch, watch, etc.)

options.name: --as
options.description: Username to impersonate for the operation

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Suppress output, only set exit code

examples: kubectl auth can-i create pods -n production

subcommands.name: certificate approve
subcommands.description: Approve a certificate signing request

options.name: --force
options.description: Update even if the CSR has been approved

options.name: --allow-missing-template-keys
options.description: Allow missing template keys in Go templates

subcommands.name: certificate deny
subcommands.description: Deny a certificate signing request

options.name: --force
options.description: Update even if the CSR has been denied

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: Create a debugging session for troubleshooting workloads and nodes

options.name: --image
options.description: Container image for the debug container

options.name: --target
options.description: Target container to share process namespace with

options.name: --copy-to
options.description: Create a copy of the target pod with this name

options.name: --set-image
options.description: Override container images in the copied pod (container=image)

options.name: --share-processes
options.description: Use shared process namespace in the copy (default true)

options.name: --same-node
options.description: Schedule the copy to the same node as the target

options.name: --env
options.description: Environment variables for the debug container

options.name: --attach
options.description: Automatically attach to the debug container

options.name: --custom
options.description: Path to a partial container spec to customize debug containers

subcommands.name: wait
subcommands.description: Wait for a specific condition on one or many resources

options.name: --for
options.description: Condition to wait for (condition=Ready, delete, jsonpath=...)

options.name: --all
options.description: Select all resources of the specified type

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Diff a local file or stdin against the live cluster state

options.name: --filename
options.description: Filename, directory, or URL to diff

options.name: --server-side
options.description: Run the diff using server-side apply

options.name: --prune-allowlist
options.description: Allowlist of resource types for pruning (when --prune is set)

subcommands.name: kustomize
subcommands.description: Build a set of KRM resources using a kustomization.yaml file

args.name: <directory>
args.description: Path to kustomization directory or URL

globalOptions.name: --context
globalOptions.description: Name of the kubeconfig context to use

globalOptions.name: --cluster
globalOptions.description: Name of the kubeconfig cluster to use

globalOptions.name: --user
globalOptions.description: Name of the kubeconfig user to use

globalOptions.name: --namespace
globalOptions.description: Kubernetes namespace scope for the request

globalOptions.name: --server
globalOptions.description: Address and port of the Kubernetes API server

globalOptions.name: --tls-server-name
globalOptions.description: Server name for TLS certificate validation

globalOptions.name: --token
globalOptions.description: Bearer token for authenticating to the API server

globalOptions.name: --request-timeout
globalOptions.description: Timeout for a single server request (e.g. 0, 5s, 2m)

globalOptions.name: --warnings-as-errors
globalOptions.description: Treat warnings from server as errors

detectors.name: current_namespace
detectors.description: Default namespace for the current context

detectors.name: pods
detectors.description: All pods across namespaces with namespace, name, and status

detectors.name: services
detectors.description: All services across namespaces with type and ports

detectors.name: deployments
detectors.description: All deployments across namespaces with ready/desired replica counts

detectors.name: nodes
detectors.description: Cluster nodes with status, roles, and kubelet version

detectors.name: secrets
detectors.description: All secrets across namespaces with their type

detectors.name: cluster_info
detectors.description: Kubernetes control plane and CoreDNS endpoint information

detectors.name: api_resources
detectors.description: All supported API resource names in the cluster

detectors.name: storage_classes
detectors.description: Available storage classes and their provisioners
